target_idiom: การทำนาบนหลังคน
source_meaning: the afterlife.
target_meaning: abstract noun of ทำนาบนหลังคน (tam-naa-bon-lǎng-kon)

target_idiom: ไก่เห็นตีนงู งูเห็นนมไก่
source_meaning: A man or woman in the workplace with whom one shares a special relationship similar to marriage, with confidences, loyalties, shared jokes and experiences, and an unusual degree of honesty or openness.
target_meaning: persons who see through each other; persons who know the secrets of each other.

target_idiom: ข้าวยากหมากแพง
source_meaning: There is little to no food or money.
target_meaning: dearth: scarcity and dearness of food.

target_idiom: เข้าด้ายเข้าเข็ม
source_meaning: To end up being successful when doing so was uncertain or unlikely.
target_meaning: Likely to be unsuccessful if interrupted by anything.

target_idiom: คงเส้นคงวา
source_meaning: steady; careful; avoiding sudden change
target_meaning: constant: consistent, invariable; unswerving, unwavering.

target_idiom: คนในอยากออก คนนอกอยากเข้า
source_meaning: A place which is very difficult to get into.
target_meaning: People inside want to get out, and people outside want to get in.

target_idiom: คารมเป็นต่อ รูปหล่อเป็นรอง
source_meaning: People who are decent, friendly, and agreeable tend to be unsuccessful because they are outmaneuvered or overwhelmed by others who are not so decent, friendly, or agreeable.
target_meaning: Those who are handsome will lose to those who are funny.

target_idiom: คาหนังคาเขา
source_meaning: in the very act; red-handed.
target_meaning: red-handed.

target_idiom: ช่วยตัวเอง
source_meaning: To masturbate.
target_meaning: to masturbate.

target_idiom: ตระบัดสัตย์
source_meaning: To make promises one cannot keep, or to make boasts that are not true.
target_meaning: to break one's word; to break one's promise.

target_idiom: นับไม่ถ้วน
source_meaning: A great many; numerous.
target_meaning: countless; numberless; innumerable.

target_idiom: บอกหนังสือสังฆราช
source_meaning: Alternative letter-case form of old school (“having conservative or traditional views on Christianity or politics”)
target_meaning: alternative form of สอนหนังสือสังฆราช (sɔ̌ɔn-nǎng-sʉ̌ʉ-sǎng-ká-râat)

source_idiom: the pen is mightier than the sword
target_idiom: ปากกาคมกว่าดาบ
source_meaning: More influence and power can be usurped by writing than by fighting.
target_meaning: the pen is mightier than the sword.

target_idiom: ผู้หญิงยิงเรือ
source_meaning: A woman; women collectively.
target_meaning: woman.

source_idiom: speech is silver, silence is golden
target_idiom: พูดไปสองไพเบี้ย นิ่งเสียตำลึงทอง
source_meaning: Not saying anything is often better than speaking too much or saying something inappropriate.
target_meaning: speech is silver, silence is golden

target_idiom: เพื่อนตาย
source_meaning: A good person, someone to be trusted; a friend.
target_meaning: true friend.

target_idiom: มักง่าย
source_meaning: Something easy or pleasant.
target_meaning: preferring convenience or ease over refinement or exertion; fond of or given to ease.

target_idiom: มือบอน
source_meaning: behaving violently or rioting
target_meaning: causing or given to vandalism

target_idiom: ไม้หลักปักขี้ควาย
source_meaning: One whose views are easily and regularly changed by the persuasion or influence of others.
target_meaning: wavering person; irresolute person; easily influenced person.

target_idiom: รำชั่วโทษพากย์
source_meaning: Alternative spelling of jacktar
target_meaning: alternative form of รำชั่วโทษพาทย์ (ram-chûua-tôot-pâat)

target_idiom: สันดอนขุดง่าย สันดานขุดยาก
source_meaning: to return to past bad habits
target_meaning: it is not easy to fix a bad habit.

source_idiom: wash one's dirty linen in public
target_idiom: สาวไส้
source_meaning: To reveal one's secrets to the public.
target_meaning: to air someone's private problems.

target_idiom: เสียงลือเสียงเล่าอ้าง
source_meaning: The rumour or news going around.
target_meaning: rumour.

target_idiom: หนอนบ่อนไส้
source_meaning: A private personal detective, employed to gather information about someone.
target_meaning: spy; inside agent; mole.

source_idiom: when they go low, we go high
target_idiom: หมากัดอย่ากัดตอบ
source_meaning: Used to indicate an unwillingness to stoop to an adversary's incivility or violence.
target_meaning: do not lower yourself to give any response to those who speak ill of you; do not fight with people of low character when they hurts you.

target_idiom: หอคอยงาช้าง
source_meaning: A sheltered, overly-academic existence or perspective, implying a disconnection or lack of awareness of reality or practical considerations.
target_meaning: ivory tower.

target_idiom: หูหนวกตาบอด
source_meaning: Utterly deaf.
target_meaning: deaf and blind: unwilling or refusing to perceive or understand; lacking perception, awareness, or judgement; not controlled by reason.

target_idiom: แหย่รังแตน
source_meaning: To cause an uproar or controversy.
target_meaning: to stir up a hornets' nest
